message:
perf(fcp-raptorq): single-buffer into_encode_all (br-gg88l)

crates/fcp-raptorq/src/encode.rs into_encode_all previously:

  1. Pre-allocated `result: Vec<(u32, Vec<u8>)>` with the final
     capacity.
  2. Collected ALL repair symbols into an intermediate
     `repairs: Vec<(u32, Vec<u8>)>` via `(0..repair_count).map(...)
     .collect()`.
  3. Moved source_data into result.
  4. `result.extend(repairs)` to drain the intermediate into result.

Even with move semantics on inner Vec<u8>s, this held a second
allocation for the tuple-slot row of every repair symbol plus the
outer Vec header. On large objects with many repairs the extra
allocation is observable.

## Fix

Destructure self at the top so we own (inner, source_data) by value,
push source symbols into result (zero clones via move), then STREAM
repair symbols straight into result:

    for i in 0..repair_count {
        let esi = k_prime.checked_add(i).expect(...);
        result.push((esi, inner.repair_symbol(esi)));
    }

No intermediate Vec. Peak memory drops from

    source_bytes + (all repairs staged) + (empty result slots)

to

    source_bytes + (one repair symbol in flight) + result-so-far.

The checked_add panic guard is preserved (bead gh7gt carry-over).

## Regression tests (2)

  into_encode_all_matches_encode_all_esi_stream
    — position-for-position ESI + bytes parity with the
      clone-based encode_all; any reorder or drop in the refactor
      surfaces here.

  into_encode_all_single_buffered_round_trip
    — encode via into_encode_all then decode via RaptorQDecoder
      and assert the payload reconstructs exactly.

## Verified

  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-swarm-p2 cargo test --offline \
    -p fcp-raptorq --lib
  -> 758 passed; 4 failed (was 756 + 2 new). The 4 pre-existing
     failures (decoder_*_duplicate / slot_cap / cancellation)
     reproduce on HEAD without these changes.

Closes flywheel_connectors-gg88l.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
